report_name: Ivanti Patches Critical Sentry Flaws Allowing Root-Level RCE (CVE-2026-10520, CVE-2026-10523)

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-10520
vulnerabilities.description: A critical, unauthenticated OS command injection vulnerability in a specific API endpoint of Ivanti Sentry that allows a remote attacker to execute commands on the underlying operating system with root privileges, leading to full system compromise.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-10523
vulnerabilities.description: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Ivanti Sentry that allows a remote attacker to create new administrative accounts on the device, which could be used for persistence or to facilitate further exploitation.

identities.name: Ivanti
identities.description: The vendor of the Sentry product who released patches for CVE-2026-10520 and CVE-2026-10523.

identities.name: WatchTowr
identities.description: The security firm that publicly released technical details and a vulnerability scanner for CVE-2026-10520.

indicators.name: Ivanti Sentry Unauthenticated RCE Attempt (CVE-2026-10520)
indicators.pattern:
title: Ivanti Sentry Unauthenticated RCE Attempt (CVE-2026-10520)
id: 7b1c8d2e-9f0a-4b3c-8d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e
status: experimental
description: Detects web requests to the vulnerable API endpoint /mifs/services/config/ on Ivanti Sentry appliances, which could indicate an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-10520 for remote code execution.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/06/10/ivanti-sentry-cve-2026-10520-cve-2026-10523/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - cve.2026.10520
level: critical
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/mifs/services/config/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests to the vulnerable API endpoint /mifs/services/config/ on Ivanti Sentry appliances, which could indicate an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-10520 for remote code execution.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process on Ivanti Sentry Appliance
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process on Ivanti Sentry Appliance
id: 8c2d9e3f-0a1b-4c4d-9e2f-3a4b5c6d7e8f
status: experimental
description: Detects the spawning of suspicious shell or network utility processes (bash, sh, nc, curl) by the Ivanti Sentry application process, which may indicate successful command injection via CVE-2026-10520.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/06/10/ivanti-sentry-cve-2026-10520-cve-2026-10523/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1059.004
  - cve.2026.10520
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentCommandLine|contains: 'sentry.jar'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/bash'
      - '/sh'
      - '/nc'
      - '/curl'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects the spawning of suspicious shell or network utility processes (bash, sh, nc, curl) by the Ivanti Sentry application process, which may indicate successful command injection via CVE-2026-10520.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Child Process on Ivanti Sentry Appliance
